Arguments are not always about who has the best logic. They are often about awareness.

Research suggests that women tend to pick up on emotional context more accurately during conflict. This includes tone, unspoken feelings, and underlying issues that may not be directly said out loud.

Being ârightâ is not about winning an argument. It is about understanding what is really happening beneath the surface and responding to the full picture, not just the words.

Sometimes intuition and emotional awareness matter just as much as facts.

1. A Milan accountant whispered: "You'll see lights in a closed restaurant on Friday â that's not dinner, that's washing." The menu's real, the guests aren't. Everything is staged for receipts.

2. The trick is genius in its simplicity. Three nights of "private events" create a full month of revenue on paper. The food never leaves the kitchen. The money becomes "service income" â taxed like catering, not capital. It appears ordinary, sounds delicious, and passes every audit.

3. These pop-ups show up all over Lombardy. Same pattern: designer interiors, short lease, staff flown in for a weekend, influencers tagged once, then silence. On books â a successful micro-restaurant. In reality â a washing machine with wine glasses.

4. "It's theater," said a chef who rented his name for one. "They don't want profit â they want paper." Each invoice clears more cash than a real kitchen could cook. When the lights go out Sunday night, the balance sheets are spotless.

5. The accountant's last line stuck: "The cleanest money in Italy smells like truffle." You'll never spot it unless you know what to look for â no lines, no bookings, just the quiet hum of a restaurant that feeds no one and pays everyone.

When you find out the Mandarin Oriental Boca developer has filed for bankruptcyâ¦again. ð« ð 

If youâve been driving past the Downtown Boca Mandarin Oriental / Via Mizner project wondering what is going on, hereâs the latest. According to @bocamag, the developer has filed for bankruptcy (again) after years of delays tied to financing challenges and the pandemic. ð³ 

The company managing the 101 Via Mizner Apartments filed for bankruptcy in January 2025. Then the company that manages the hotel portion filed for Chapter 11 in December 2025 due to $155 million+ in debt. ðµ

The interesting part? Despite lawsuits from some condo buyers and refunded deposits, the condo portion is reportedly nearly sold out and the project could still open this year. Wow.

Do you notice every shift in your partner's tone? ð­

If a delayed text or a subtle change in mood sends your mind spiraling, you're not broken. Research suggests this may be a sign of an anxious attachment style.

Here's what the science says: 

Attachment theory proposes that our early bonds with caregivers shape how we connect in adult relationships. If a caregiver was inconsistent with affection or availability, we may have learned to stay hypervigilant, constantly scanning for any sign that connection might be threatened. ð§ 

This means small changes, like a partner seeming distant or a short reply, can activate a deep fear of rejection or abandonment. The emotional response can feel overwhelming, but studies suggest it's not a character flaw. It's a learned survival strategy your nervous system developed to protect the relationship. ð

Understanding this pattern can be a meaningful first step toward building more secure connections. Working with a therapist who specializes in attachment may help you recognize these triggers and develop new responses over time. ð¤

This awareness supports emotional wellbeing, but healing is a process and results vary by individual. There's no quick fix here, just consistent, compassionate work.

If this resonates, know that your sensitivity is not weakness. It's your brain trying to keep you safe in the only way it learned how. That deserves understanding, not judgment. â¤ï¸

You're not "too much." You're human. ð§¬

Sources: Levine et al., Attached (2010)

Golf Digest â Llama caddies? Llama caddies! No need to mention anything else. Letâs just get right into it.

Sherwood Forest Golf Club in Brevard, North Carolina, has been pushing this, ahem, new type of caddie for some time now, but according to Axios, âtourism and recreational activities involving llamas have been on an upswing,â and these camelid loopers are as popular as it gets. There are around 30-ish llamas at the course that have been trained to carry your bags. Itâs just as adorable as you might expect.

The caddies donât need any cash either. Theyâre fine with some water and âa handful of tasty greens.â

Historically, llamas have been used to carry heavy things and can typically lug anything between 70 to 120 pounds. Theyâre from the Andes Mountain regions of Peru, Bolivia, and Chile and are known as âeasy walkers,â so as to not destroy the grass they tread on, according to the Rutgers 4-H Animal Science Resource Blog.

Llamas as caddies have been around since the early 1990s when Talamore Golf Resort in Southern Pines used the idea to generate publicity at the newly opened Rees Jones-designed course. It was a hit and is now being replicated by Mark English, a golfer and owner of a nearby llama farm, who threw the idea out to Sherwood club pro Brian Lautenschlager.

Golfers are assigned their own llama, along with an expert to escort each foursome. Sorry to all of the human caddies out there, but this might be the future of golf. It may be time to head back to college for all the loopers out there to pick up an animal science degree. The game is constantly moving. You donât want to fall behind.

#TheBananasPodcast #LlamazingCaddie #AlpacaYourClubs

ð¯ On this day in Boston historyâ¦

On January 15, 1919, the Great Molasses Flood shocked the North End. A massive tank holding more than 2.3 million gallons of molasses burst without warning, sending a sticky wave racing through the streets at speeds said to reach 35 mph.

The disaster took 21 people's lives and injured over 150, leaving behind one of Bostonâs strangest and most tragic stories. For decades, locals swore that on hot summer days, the neighborhood still smelled faintly of molasses.

Boston history is wild.

Processed deli meats, including deli ham, are classified by the World Health Organization as Group 1 carcinogens, meaning there is strong evidence linking them to cancer, in the same evidence category as tobacco and asbestos.

Health risks linked to deli ham:
	â¢	Cancer risk: Regular intake of processed meat, even around 50 grams per day (about two slices), has been associated with higher rates of colorectal cancer. Some research also suggests links to increased breast and prostate cancer risk.
	â¢	Heart disease and diabetes: A 2025 study published in Nature Medicine reported no clearly safe level of processed meat consumption, finding associations with higher rates of type 2 diabetes and ischemic heart disease even at low intakes.
	â¢	High sodium and saturated fat: Deli ham often contains far more sodium than fresh pork and higher levels of saturated fat, contributing to high blood pressure, elevated LDL cholesterol, and stroke risk.

Why deli ham raises concern:
	â¢	Nitrates and nitrites: These preservatives can convert into nitrosamines in the body, compounds known to damage the colon lining and increase cancer risk.
	â¢	Ultra-processing: Many deli meats are made from reconstituted meat paste and include additives such as carrageenan, maltodextrin, and corn syrup solids, which are associated with metabolic and inflammatory concerns.

Whatâs your take on this?

Courtship among cephalopods comes with a very real chance of death. A male octopus that approaches the wrong female or misjudges her mood is not met with gentle refusal. The response is direct, forceful, and in many cases deadly.

Researchers have observed females hurling objects at males they do not want, overpowering them with their arms, or consuming them outright. In this environment, a failure to read social cues does not lead to disappointment. It leads to becoming part of the food chain.

Run to @fspalmbeach because the legendary @handshake_bar from Mexico City has officially landed in South Florida for a limited time speakeasy pop up through January 3 ð¸ð´

Recently ranked #1 on the Worldâs 50 Best Bars list, Handshake brings its boundary pushing, story driven cocktail experience to the Four Seasons Palm Beach and it is next level. Every drink is intentional, every bite is elevated, and nothing here is by accident. From the experimental on site lab to cocktails that take days to perfect, this is not your average night out.

What we loved:

ð¥ Crab croissants with caviar
ð The iconic Butter Mushroom Old Fashioned
ð¦ Grasshoppers and nuts bar snack (yes, we tried it!)
ð¸ Cocktails unlike anything weâve had before

This is a full experience from start to finish and way more than just grabbing a drink. The vibes, the creativity, the team, it all delivers.

If you are in Palm Beach for the holidays, do not miss this before they leave Florida. Trust us.

We want to take a moment to sincerely thank everyone who has reached out, checked in, and shared so much love and support over the past days. It has meant more than words can express.

Our beloved leader, Jose Gonzalez, is deeply missed. 
He is not only an essential part of the BiCE family, but someone who has touched so many lives through his dedication, kindness, and heart.

We remain hopeful that he will be home with us very soon. Until then, he has our full and unwavering support - from the BiCE family, his loved ones, and this incredible community that continues to lift him up.

We truly believe that together, with unity and compassion, we will get through this. Thank you for standing with us. ð¤
